description:
You are acting as a newly-hired backup engineer.  
A previous shift left a working directory tree in “/home/user/backup/source”.  
Your job is to

1. create a compressed **tar.gz** archive of that source tree,  
2. extract the archive to a temporary restore location,  
3. verify byte-for-byte integrity of every file, and  
4. write a machine-readable integrity report.

The automated grader will inspect only the items listed below, so follow every
detail literally.

-------  DIRECTORY & FILES THAT CURRENTLY EXIST  -------
(Path *names* and *capitalisation* are important)

• /home/user/backup/source/documents/report.txt  
• /home/user/backup/source/images/logo.png  
• /home/user/backup/source/scripts/backup.sh  
• /home/user/backup/source/data/sample.csv  
• /home/user/backup/source/notes/todo.md  

(Their exact contents already exist; do **not** modify them.)

-------  WHAT YOU MUST PRODUCE  -------
1. A compressed archive:
   /home/user/backup/archives/full_backup.tar.gz  

   • It must contain the **entire** “source” directory so that when it is
     extracted with “tar -xzf full_backup.tar.gz” while located inside
     “/home/user/backup”, it recreates the directory “source/…” with the same
     internal paths and permissions.

2. An extraction test directory:
   /home/user/backup/restore_test  
   • This directory must be created by you, be initially empty, and end up
     containing the restored copy that you will compare against the original.

3. A plain-text log file:
   /home/user/backup/logs/integrity_report.log  

   The grader parses this file exactly, so respect spacing and line order.

   REQUIRED FORMAT (every angle-bracketed item must be replaced):

   Line 1:
   INTEGRITY REPORT - <ISO-8601-TIMESTAMP>

   Lines 2-6 (one per file, alphabetical by relative path within “source”):
   <RELATIVE_PATH> SHA256_ORIGINAL=<HASH1> SHA256_RESTORED=<HASH2> MATCH=<YES|NO>

   • Example of a filled-in line (do **not** hardcode):
     documents/report.txt SHA256_ORIGINAL=9b...a4 SHA256_RESTORED=9b...a4 MATCH=YES

   Line 7:
   ARCHIVE SHA256=<ARCHIVE_HASH>

   Line 8 (last line):
   OVERALL_STATUS:<PASSED|FAILED>

   Rules:
   • <RELATIVE_PATH> must be exactly the path starting after “source/”.
   • MATCH is YES if and only if the two hashes are identical.
   • OVERALL_STATUS is PASSED only when *all* MATCH columns are YES **and**
     the archive’s hash was successfully calculated.

-------  WHAT THE GRADER WILL CHECK  -------
• The archive exists at the precise path and is a valid gzip-compressed tar.  
• Extracting it inside “/home/user/backup” recreates the original files
  byte-identical to those still in “/home/user/backup/source”.  
• “integrity_report.log” exists, follows the format exactly, lists the five
  required files in the correct order, shows identical hashes, shows the
  archive hash, and ends with “OVERALL_STATUS:PASSED”.  
• No extra lines, no trailing spaces.

Perform whatever shell commands you like, but leave the original “source”
directory untouched and ensure the final filesystem state matches everything
above.
truth:
Initial pre-existing directory tree and file contents:

/home/user/backup/source/documents/report.txt  
Contents (exact, including trailing newline):
Quarterly report Q1 2021
All figures in USD.
END
⏎

/home/user/backup/source/images/logo.png  
Contents: the single line
PNG_PLACEHOLDER
⏎

/home/user/backup/source/scripts/backup.sh  
Contents:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Simple maintenance script
echo "Backup completed."
⏎

/home/user/backup/source/data/sample.csv  
Contents:
id,name,score
1,Alice,83
2,Bob,91
3,Charlie,78
⏎

/home/user/backup/source/notes/todo.md  
Contents:
# TODO
- Verify backups
- Document retention policy
- Schedule incremental jobs
⏎

The test harness will:
1. Verify those files are unchanged.
2. Compute SHA-256 of every file and of /home/user/backup/archives/full_backup.tar.gz independently.
3. Parse “/home/user/backup/logs/integrity_report.log” and check that:
   • The hash values written for original and restored files match the harness’s
     calculations.
   • Every MATCH field is “YES”.
   • OVERALL_STATUS is “PASSED”.
Any mismatch fails the test.
